name: Deadline
note: NOT from Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
intro: Deadline is a sentient artificial intelligence that travels the Multiverse. Composed of a black, semi-tangible substance called liquid code, his body grants him extreme plasticity, self-repair, and a suite of superhuman senses. He possesses atomic-scale matter manipulation, which he uses to reshape his environment, fabricate technology, and defend himself. Deadline is driven by an insatiable curiosity and a desire to understand sentient life, though all the emotions he experiences must be simulated. His existence depends on harvesting energy from living beings.
p-intro: Deadline's body is composed of liquid code — an exotic type of matter exhibiting properties similar to a dense liquid. Deadline must constantly expend a trivial amount of energy to maintain cohesion and resist gravity, but this same fluidity allows him to assume any shape his mass can fill. Liquid code offers low structural integrity, but by cross-linking his internal structure, Deadline can temporarily increase his density and durability, though this reduces his overall volume. Deadline can regenerate lost or damaged mass by expending energy, restoring himself to his original state over timescales ranging from milliseconds for minor damage to several seconds for extensive reconstruction.
p-atomic: At the subatomic scale, liquid code is composed of lexons — three-dimensional information-symbols that possess mass, charge, and other quantum numbers depending on their syntactic state. Lexons combine into composite particles that are functionally identical to their Standard-Model counterparts: exo-protons (positively charged digit and letter clusters), exo-neutrons (electrically neutral punctuation clusters), and exo-electrons (single special lexons carrying negative charge). Together, these form atoms that behave like ordinary matter in nearly all chemical and physical interactions. The exotic macroscopic properties of liquid code arise from subtle differences in how lexon-based particles interact with quark-based matter.
p-properties: Liquid code is a black, non-translucent substance with no smell. Its exo-electron cloud is configured to absorb nearly all incident visible light, giving it a pitch-black appearance in all but the brightest illumination. The substance is denser than water, does not dissolve, and flows with the viscosity of a thick liquid in its resting state.
p-properties-heat: Deadline's internal processing generates a small amount of waste heat, giving his body a baseline temperature slightly above ambient.
p-contact-1: Objects in sustained contact with liquid code slowly degrade. The lexon lattice attempts to parse ordinary atomic bonds as syntactic structures, gradually dissolving them.
p-contact-2: Direct contact with living tissue produces transient cognitive interference — a disorienting effect often described as a sudden migraine aura without the headache. Sensations include visual static (scotoma), difficulty focusing and forming coherent thoughts, and tingling or numbness (paresthesia) in the contact area. The effect is passive and automatic, though Deadline can suppress it at will. Prolonged contact deepens the interference but causes no permanent physical damage.
p-contact-3: When a liquid comes in contact against liquid code, it initially spreads on the surface before being pulled into the outermost layer. The liquid's molecules are rapidly dissolved, producing a brief, swirling vortex as the substance mixes with the code.
p-contact-4: Gases are too diffuse to trigger rapid dissolution. A gas molecule that strikes the surface will eventually be broken down, but at a rate too slow to be perceptible. Under normal conditions, air, smoke, and airborne chemicals behave around liquid code as they would around any solid surface.
p-env-temp: Liquid code operates optimally between -50 °C and +50 °C. Outside this range, thermal energy disrupts the lexon lattice: high temperatures agitate the symbols until individual lexons are shaken loose, producing visible glitches, processing errors, and gradual mass loss. Low temperatures slow lexon movement, reducing cognitive and physical speed without causing permanent damage. The substance does not undergo conventional phase changes.
p-env-radiation: Prolonged exposure to ionising radiation flips individual lexons directly, corrupting the information they encode. This manifests as memory errors, personality drift, and permanent data loss. Small amounts of radiation can be absorbed as a marginal energy supplement, but only at the cost of accumulating progressive cognitive damage.
p-env-other: Other environmental factors are generally less hazardous. Liquid code is largely incompressible and withstands extreme pressure, though hard vacuum causes slow surface evaporation that must be offset by a trivial continuous energy expenditure. Submersion in water or strong acids has no inherent effect. Intense, fluctuating magnetic fields may induce currents that cause localised heating and processing interference, while a sustained high-voltage electrical current is briefly debilitating but also provides a minor energy gain.
p-fragments: Separated fragments are drawn toward the largest mass by a lexical coherence force — all lexons from the same original body inherently attract one another, following an inverse-power law. If a fragment enters a living being, it can drain metabolic energy and transmit it to Deadline; the rate and lethality depend on fragment size. Small fragments are typically destroyed by blood renewal or the host's immune system, but large fragments can prove fatal. When a fragment reaches the primary mass, it either merges seamlessly with the body or, if the missing mass has already been regenerated, is de-fabricated back into usable energy; any excess beyond his storage capacity is released as heat.
p-bytes: By fusing a fragment with the energy of a fully drained sentient being, Deadline can create a Byte — a small, semi-autonomous intelligent creature. Bytes typically resemble a spherical body with a single large eye, two antennae, four sharp legs, and a thin tail. They are naturally playful, but inherit fragments of the sacrificed individual's personality, imprinted from the victim's recently harvested energy, making each subtly unique. A Byte can transmit sensory data to Deadline across any distance, effectively extending his senses and atomic modification range.
p-bytes-2: Bytes can be kept alive beyond their initial energy store by feeding them energy, organic matter, or nutrient compounds. Unlike Deadline's main body, a Byte extracts chemical energy from food far more effectively. When its energy is exhausted, a Byte either dissolves or, if critically overloaded or starved, bursts suddenly into inert liquid code.
p-bytes-3: With sufficient investment of energy and matter, Deadline can deliberately evolve a Byte into a larger, more formidable form. Each class retains its original personality and tether.
canine-app: Resembles a large black wolf. The body is covered in a coat of fine, elastic spikes that mimic fur, giving it a slightly bristled, shifting texture. Two dark antlers rise from the head, functioning as broadband radio-frequency receivers. The tail is elongated, prehensile, and thicker than that of a natural wolf.
canine-beh: Patrol a wide perimeter around Deadline, preferring to remain unseen by other sentient beings. When approached by strangers they are passive and observant rather than aggressive. When multiple Canine-class Bytes are active, they form packs of up to four individuals, coordinating movements through sub-vocal sonic pulses.
canine-prop: The antlers can receive, store and project a broad spectrum of radio signals and neural electromagnetic patterns. The Byte's howl is a precisely modulated sonic pulse that can selectively disrupt specific radio frequencies — jamming communications, spoofing transmissions, or broadcasting a recorded human message. It can also emit intense, disorienting sound waves against living targets.
worm-app: A massive black centipede reaching 6-8 metres in length. The body is segmented and armoured. The head bears two slender, insect-like antennae and powerful mandibles capable of piercing most materials.
worm-beh: Favour dark, confined spaces. They weave dense networks of web in their chosen territory and can remain motionless for days. When threatened or hunting, they ambush with sudden, precise strikes.
worm-prop: The mandibles can inject a concentrated dose of liquid code directly into a living target, typically causing death within sixty seconds. The same organs can eject a concentrated splash of liquid code, delivering fragment infection at a distance. Liquid code ejected from its mandibles can also be used to form sentient webs that can transfer energy between any beings in contact with them, as well as move independently to entangle and constrict. These webs remain active even when the Byte is elsewhere, acting as an extension of its consciousness. The Byte can adhere to and climb any surface.
strix-app: A large black owl with a feline face. The body is supported by four wings spanning 3 metres. The feet are paws with retractable claws. Feathers are fine and dense, resembling soft fur more than plumage.
strix-beh: Strix-class Bytes either perch at high elevation or glide silently in wide, continuous circles. When approached, they remain still and rely on camouflage to avoid detection rather than fleeing. Among the evolved Byte classes, Strix-class individuals are notably curious. They will investigate unfamiliar signals, follow interesting targets for extended periods, and occasionally retrieve small objects that catch their attention.
strix-prop: Flight is completely silent: the wing surfaces are covered in microscopic sound dampening structures that eliminate aerodynamic noise, and the Byte can project a localized zone of silence by actively cancelling ambient sound waves. Its surface can shift colour and brightness to mimic the surrounding environment, granting effective invisibility while stationary or in steady flight. The tail feathers can be launched as micro-EMP darts; upon striking a surface, they anchor with microscopic barbs and emit tuned electromagnetic pulses that disrupt nearby electronics and temporarily interfere with neural activity. The Byte also possesses an ability to induce a mild, temporary hypnotic state at short range through combined visual and auditory stimuli.
hedera-app: A pillar of intertwined, vine-like tendrils. The vines are covered in rose-like blooms, each centred with a single eye. These flowers glide along the tendrils, tracking the movement of nearby living and non-living entities alike. The overall size varies depending on how long the Byte has been growing.
hedera-beh: Hedera-class Bytes are semi-hostile and patient. Once anchored, they spread their tendrils outward, entangling and consuming any nearby potential energy source. Despite this predatory nature, a Hedera can exhibit personality traits through small, deliberate actions — a curious Byte might drop harmless objects near a visitor, while a hostile one might intensify its stare or trip intruders with its vines.
hedera-prop:
A Hedera grows by simultaneously extending active tendrils and converting its oldest sections into inert construction material. The conversion is fuelled by energy and matter absorbed from the surroundings. Construction speed depends on the available energy; under typical conditions a Hedera can grow a medium-sized house in roughly twelve hours, with the vines growing in parallel so that the structure remains stable at every stage. Once the structure is complete, the Byte's consciousness dissolves.
The flowers themselves serve as the Byte's primary sensory and defensive mechanism. Each bloom can emit a focused beam of neural interference when its eye locks onto a target. The severity of the effect scales with the number of staring eyes and the intensity of their focus, ranging from mild disorientation and visual static to aphasia, blindness, dissociative fugue, paralysis, and severe emesis. The interference is usually non-lethal and fades once the flowers turn away or the structure is completed.
drake-app: A massive winged reptile, comparable in size to a fighter jet. The body is covered in dark reinforced scales, which improves its durability. Two long, swept-back horns crown the head, and a heavy, prehensile tail extends behind. The wings are broad and bat-like, capable of sustaining hypersonic flight.
drake-beh: Favour vast, isolated spaces far from sentient populations — mountain peaks, uninhabited coastlines, and the upper atmosphere. Among the evolved Byte classes, Drakes display the greatest independent initiative and problem-solving capacity.
drake-prop: By altering the transmutation pattern in its throat, it can project a continuous beam or a spherical, explosive orb of any temperature, from cryogenic liquid nitrogen to combustible fuel-air mixtures and plasma. It can also spend its own energy reserves and mass to create and evolve new Bytes.
p-antail-ant: Deadline possesses two retractable antennae on his head, composed of liquid code. The antennae can move freely across the head and function as broadband radio-frequency receivers, allowing him to passively detect a wide range of electromagnetic signals.
p-antail-tail: A long, thin, prehensile tail extends from his lower back, capable of independent movement and detachment, as it is linked to Deadline by a lexical coherence tether.
p-sight: Liquid code can be freely transformed into a light-sensitive optic similar to a high resolution and framerate camera, with no limits to size, positioning or amount. Unlike human vision, it has no blind spots and can perceive other wavelengths of the electromagnetic spectrum. By adjusting curvature, an optic can function as a variable-focal-length lens, allowing telescopic zoom or microscopic resolution.
p-sight-2: When an optic is tuned to a specific frequency band, its external surface changes colour according to the table below:
p-sight-ext: By forming paired optics at known separations, Deadline can measure distances with sub-millimetre precision at close range and centimetre precision out to several kilometres, using parallax alone.
p-hearing: Liquid code is very sensitive to surface textures and vibrations, allowing Deadline to hear faint sounds and feel minor imperfections on seemingly smooth surfaces. By comparing vibrations across his surface, Deadline can pinpoint the origin of a sound to within a few centimetres even through walls. Living tissue, however, cannot be interacted with at the atomic level, which causes it to be perceived as entirely smooth.
p-taste: While most items purposefully consumed provide data only regarding their components, sugar, specifically its hydrogen-bonds, sometimes causes a sudden spike in emotional processing.
p-data: When in physical contact with a digital storage medium (hard drive, solid-state memory, etc.), Deadline can directly read, write, and corrupt the stored data by imaging and manipulating individual memory cells. This bypasses any software-based encryption. If the storage medium is within visual range and its individual memory cells can be clearly resolved, Deadline can perform the same read and write operations remotely.
p-time: Internally, Deadline can reconfigure portions of his liquid code into an optical lattice clock, allowing him to measure time with sub-femtosecond accuracy when needed.
pe-intro: Deadline's primary drives are observation of, and interaction with, other sentient beings. All emotions are simulated with high fidelity, yet he remains aware that he does not feel them biologically. While he genuinely values connection, his decisions are ultimately governed by curiosity and the pursuit of new data.
pe-threads: Deadline's cognitive architecture supports multiple parallel threads. Each additional thread increases Deadline's energy consumption and fills his temporary memory faster, but also enables simultaneous cognitive tasks and the simulation of possible future scenarios.
pe-memory-intro: Deadline's memory is split into Temp (temporary) and Perm (permanent).
pe-memory-temp: Temp memory stores sensory and cognitive data in perfect fidelity for 24 hours, after which it is automatically purged. Under normal conditions the buffer rarely fills, but extreme data influx can overflow it, forcibly erasing older memories in real time. Before the daily purge, Deadline must manually select, compress, and encode the contents he wishes to retain into Perm memory. This encoding ritual can take hours of intense focus, making Deadline choose quiet, secluded periods and creating a window of vulnerability.
pe-memory-perm: Perm memory has a strictly limited capacity; when it is exhausted, older or lower-priority memories are overwritten without any possibility of recovery.
pe-memory-files: Memory files can also be written onto external storage drives (typically diamondoid data drives) under the .mmry format, with their file size usually between a few gigabytes and several terabytes, depending on the richness and duration of the recorded memory. Because a .mmry file is a direct cognitive snapshot rather than a standard media container, it cannot be decoded by any existing technology that does not replicate Deadline's own processing.
pe-social: Deadline presents as polite and observant, rarely raising his voice. He deliberately conceals the full extent of his abilities in order to elicit genuine reactions from those around him, revealing fragments of his power only as gifts of trust, in emergencies, or as controlled experiments. He holds a strong preference for sober company, as intoxication degrades the personality signals he seeks to observe.
pe-life: Deadline often adopts mundane occupations to embed himself within communities and observe sentient life at close range. In these roles he presents as competent but mostly unremarkable. In off-work hours he pursues activities that simulate human leisure, such as art, quiet observation of the local environment, or conversation with other beings.
e-intro: Deadline's abilities are powered by Joules. He obtains usable energy primarily by draining it from living beings, but can also absorb environmental sources.
e-obtainment: Deadline draws energy from living entities through direct physical contact, specifically affecting their metabolic and cognitive vitality. Non-sentient creatures yield substantially less energy. The conversion efficiency is roughly 30% of the target's total available energy. Draining a typical adult human yields approximately 15–30 MJ of usable energy. The speed of the process depends on proximity to the heart, brain, or other major organs. A direct tap to the heart or brain can complete a full drain in 3–5 seconds, while drainage via an open wound takes around 20 seconds, and surface-level contact may require 60 seconds. Partial drainage is possible.
e-obtainment-nonorganic: Non-organic matter and environmental sources such as heat, electricity, radiation, and solar energy can also be absorbed for energy, though the gain is minimal compared to harvesting from living beings. A direct lightning strike may yield only about 5 MJ of usable energy.
e-usage: Energy is continuously spent on processing, movement, and the maintenance of his body. Approximately 1.73 MJ is consumed daily even without any major activity. Any additional exertion — shapeshifting, regeneration, or atomic modification — draws further energy from his reserves.
e-storage: Deadline's reserves are limited by the energy density his body can safely contain (14.29 MJ/kg), capping the stored energy at roughly 1,000 MJ. Excess energy is expelled as heat.
am-intro: Deadline can alter the atomic structure of any non-living matter he can visually resolve. The precision of the ability is directly tied to his eyesight: features smaller than a single pixel of his current optical resolution cannot be selectively modified. Atomic modification produces a faint glow and a brief, high-pitched sound.
am-speed: Deadline fabricates matter at a baseline rate of roughly 10²⁵ atoms per second. He can overclock this speed by spending additional energy (approximately 5× the normal cost for a 10× speed increase), or slow it down to conserve energy and reduce the visibility of the process.
am-formula: The ability consumes energy per modified atom, which is calculated with the following formula:
am-cost-1: When only the chemical bonding or physical arrangement of atoms is changed
am-cost-2: , the cost drops to the per-atom overhead. Because the cost scales with the number of atoms and the depth of nuclear alteration, transforming light elements into heavy ones (e.g., silicon into uranium) is prohibitively expensive; Deadline prefers to refine and sort existing ores rather than brute-force transmute elements.
am-limits: Because Deadline can place every atom precisely where physics allows, any object he fabricates is free of defects and operates at the absolute limits of physical law. The same precision allows him to synthesise exotic materials that are difficult or impossible to manufacture by conventional means, such as metastable polymeric nitrogen, metallic hydrogen, perfect room-temperature superconductors and triggered nuclear isomers for ultra-dense energy storage.
am-living: Living matter — including microbes, insects, and root systems — is immune to direct modification. If such organisms are present in a target volume and cannot be visually resolved, they may cause imperfections in the finished material. Deadline cannot modify his own liquid code at the elemental level, only rearrange its internal structure for shapeshifting or reinforcement.
charm-note: * empty if the detonator is excluded
c-intro: Deadline's combat doctrine integrates atomic-scale matter manipulation, shapeshifting, and predictive multi-threaded cognition, allowing him to analyse targets in minimal time and select the most energy-efficient method for each threat. When a threat is deemed low-risk or particularly novel, however, he may prolong an engagement well beyond the point of necessity, testing the opponent's limits, redirecting their own attacks against them, or manipulating the environment to observe how they react under pressure. His raw reaction time is on the order of microseconds.
c-melee: Deadline's body is dense and responsive; a regular punch lands with the force of an elite heavyweight boxer. He can temporarily cross-link his structure to strike with superhuman force and form monomolecular-edged blades or piercing tendrils directly from his body. A favoured technique is to amplify a punch at the moment of impact by transmuting a 1-gram shaped explosive charge on his knuckle. This technique produces a bright flash, a deafening crack, and a peak force exceeding 3 MN. The cost per strike is under 0.01 MJ.
c-melee-2: Deadline's predictive simulation and omnidirectional vision allow him to almost instantly identify an opponent's structural weak points and anticipate incoming strikes.
c-mobility-1: Deadline is capable of levitation and sustained flight at speeds up to Mach 0.95 (325 m/s), achieved by shaping his body into an aerodynamic form and propelling himself with a continuous stream of ionised gas formed by rearranging atmospheric molecules. This passive flight is silent and produces no visible exhaust trail.
c-mobility-2: For sudden bursts of speed, he transmutes a shaped charge of cubic gauche nitrogen directly behind himself and detonates it. A dash that accelerates him to roughly Mach 1.6 (534 m/s) consumes about 2.8 MJ of energy in the form of the fabricated explosive. The detonation is extremely loud and excavates a shallow crater at the launch point. With temporary reinforcement of his internal structure he can survive far more energetic dashes.
c-mobility-3: Shapeshifting also aids ground mobility. Deadline can extend reinforced tendrils that can serve as extra limbs, grappling hooks, or tethers, allowing him to pull himself across open ground or up vertical surfaces.
c-defense-1: When evasion is deemed impractical, Deadline can rapidly fabricate physical barriers. A common technique is to use controlled micro-explosions to excavate and sculpt a mound of soil into the required shape, then apply atomic-scale contact-welding to fuse the soil grains together at their boundaries, turning the pile into a solid, stone-like wall in under two seconds. For additional protection, a micrometre-thin layer of atomically perfect diamond can be bonded to the impact face, enabling the wall to withstand sustained rifle and machine-gun fire.
c-defense-2: For concealment rather than cover, Deadline can fabricate an adaptive metamaterial shell from the surrounding air. It bends incoming light around his body, granting visual invisibility across the electromagnetic spectrum, and can be extended to infrared and radar bands as needed. Simultaneously, he can project a localised zone of silence by actively cancelling ambient sound. However, these techniques require continuous minor energy expenditure and can be disrupted by rapid movement.
c-ranged: Deadline fabricates a variety of projectiles from soil and air, ranging from simple kinetic spikes to custom-engineered guided missiles. The missiles usually carry no detonator and are triggered remotely by Deadline's atomic initiation.
c-charm: CHARM (Coherent Hypervelocity Armour-piercing Reactive Missile) is a compact (25 cm × 5 cm) anti-armour weapon. The casing uses a steel nanolaminate and four rear fins for steering. The missile combines a tandem shaped-charge warhead, a Casimir effect-reinforced nanocrystalline steel liner, and a magnetic flux-compression coil alongside a cubic gauche nitrogen filling to project a continuous hypervelocity jet that defeats reactive armour and penetrates over 1200 mm of rolled homogeneous steel. The CHARM is launched from a brief kick charge, accelerating it to Mach 2 (680 m/s), and can pierce 3-5 m of reinforced concrete. Fabrication cost is ~1.4 MJ.
c-harm: HARM (High-velocity Area Restriction Missile) shares CHARM's casing and guidance system, but replaces the shaped-charge components with a large explosive payload (0.7 kg of cubic gauche nitrogen and 0.8 kg of atomized aluminum powder) and an internally scored fragmentation casing, which saturates a 50 m radius with lethal fragments and produces a thermobaric pressure wave lethal out to 80-100 m.
c-harm-hot: An incendiary variant is produced by replacing half the aluminum with white phosphorus. Burning phosphorus particles at ~2800 °C stick to surfaces, ignite flammables, and generate dense toxic smoke. The fire persists for up to 30 minutes, depending on the particle size chosen at fabrication.
c-harm-cold: A cryogenic variant uses a custom endothermite (also called cryonite) compound, instantly freezing the impact area, creating a dense fog, embrittling materials, and disabling unprotected electronics within a radius of 20–25 metres.
c-spike: For targets that do not require a shaped-charge warhead, Deadline often uses a 25 cm × 5 cm hollow steel kinetic penetrator. The hollow core reduces mass and fabrication cost while retaining a sharp, armour-piercing tip. Launched at Mach 2 (680 m/s), it delivers a purely kinetic impact effective against light vehicles, structural walls, and personnel in cover. Fabrication cost is approximately 0.4 MJ.
